danger_signals.visual_form: portrait becomes progressively battered and bloody
danger_signals.meaning: visual indicator of opponent health state and danger level to player

danger_signals.visual_form: george_foreman portrait becomes progressively battered and bloody
danger_signals.meaning: visual indicator of player health state; increased damage means higher risk of knockout

danger_signals.visual_form: opponent knocked to canvas, referee begins 10-count
danger_signals.meaning: critical danger to opponent; accumulation of knockdowns triggers round/match loss conditions

safety_signals.visual_form: blocking animation plays when player executes defensive maneuver
safety_signals.meaning: mitigation of incoming damage; indicates safe defensive positioning

safety_signals.visual_form: character dodge animation in two directions
safety_signals.meaning: avoidance of damage; safe positioning relative to opponent

safety_signals.visual_form: impact animation when player's punch connects
safety_signals.meaning: offensive control; player maintaining safe pressure on opponent

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: boxing ring; player occupies left/center position by convention

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: different opponent portraits per match in career mode
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: boxing ring; opponent occupies right/center position by convention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: both fighters shown in portraits with clean/undamaged appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: both boxers at full energy; opponent movement patterns normal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: portraits show progressive bruising, swelling, blood; visible damage increases with each round
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent movement may become erratic; player must manage stamina

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: severe facial damage on portrait; opponent on canvas during 10-count sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent immobilized; referee counting; if reaches fourth knockdown or third in single round, match ends

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: both fighters standing; judge decision screen displays punch statistics, knockdowns, damage metrics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: match ends by decision; winner determined by aggregate stats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: portrait gallery of opponents increases; circuit titles accumulate visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent difficulty escalates; more complex attack patterns; harder to evade

environmental_storytelling.example: boxer's face shifts from clean to bruised to heavily battered across rounds, telling story of combat intensity without exposition
career mode structure of uniting three fictional circuit belts creates narrative arc of progression toward undisputed heavyweight championship
sequence of increasingly tough opponents tells story of rising challenge and player skill acquisition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: standard attack pattern; predictable punch sequences; normal evasion responses
movement becomes less predictable; erratic attack timing; defensive patterns weaken
brief stagger animation before resuming combat; momentary vulnerability window
limited mobility increases; dodge range may diminish; punch recovery time lengthens
enters ring; begins audible/visible 10-count sequence; halts opponent action

notes: George Foreman's KO Boxing uses minimal visual language typical of early 1990s Genesis boxing games. The primary manifestation system is the progressive portrait damage mechanic, which communicates world-state through facial injury accumulation rather than elaborate animation. The game lacks faction complexity (single-player vs. opponent structure) and environmental sophistication. World-state communication is almost entirely reliant on: (1) health/portrait damage visualization, (2) knockdown status, (3) victory condition displays (KO/TKO/decision). No faction signaling, color identity, or political narrative exists. The game is mechanically deterministic with limited emergent storytelling—danger and safety are purely mechanical (damage values, knockdown counters) rendered through sprite-based portraiture.
